rationale.R1.1: Artifact contains an explicit contribution claim ('first cross-lingual sentiment classifier') and a gap statement (translation-drift critique); checking whether these are coherent and defensible is naturally in scope for any 'check'.
rationale.R1.2: Logical tension exists between claiming primacy and simultaneously citing Smith et al. (2022) and Liu et al. (2019) for the same core ideas; consistency of premises-to-conclusions needs examination.
rationale.R1.3: Specific accuracy numbers (81.3%, 76.1%, etc.) are asserted with reference to an absent Table 4; verifying that claims are grounded in presented evidence is central to any check of this excerpt.
rationale.R1.4: The word 'first' and 'novel' appear alongside prior-work citations that arguably cover the same ground; novelty delta vs. nearest prior art is a core review concern.
rationale.R1.6: Sentence-level polish is a plausible primary intent of 'check this' on a prose excerpt; included as central though it may not be load-bearing depending on clarification.
rationale.R1.7: Two citations (Smith et al. 2022; Liu et al. 2019) are invoked with substantive claims about what each showed; attribution accuracy and coverage of related work warrant scrutiny.

parsed.clarification_question: Should I focus on technical and logical validity (logical consistency, evidence-claim alignment, novelty vs. prior work, citation accuracy), or on writing quality and proofreading only?
